title: 悬崖上的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\241-悬崖上的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Frankie visits Dr. Nicholson to invite his wife, Moira, to stay at her home for safety. Dr. Nicholson claims that Moira left early that morning for London, possibly to stay at the Savoy Hotel to seek stimulation. Frankie finds the doctor's polite yet sarcastic demeanor suspicious. After leaving, Frankie informs her driver, Bobby, that she believes Nicholson is lying and that Moira may have been missing since the previous night. Bobby expresses regret for previously allowing Moira to return home but maintains she must still be alive, suggesting she has been secretly moved or is still in Granchester. To avoid suspicion in Merrow, Bobby instructs Frankie to return to London to trace the Kemm family while he moves to Ambledon to use as a base of operations to find Moira.

In London, Frankie begins investigating the Kemms by visiting real estate agencies. At Gordon and Porter Real Estate Company, an agent informs her that Mr. Kemann rented 17 St. Leonard's Garden in Paddington for a quarter but has since departed, possibly to the Hilton Tower at Paddington Station. Frankie lies, claiming Kemann owes her money, to explain her interest. She then visits a second agency, where she successfully obtains a viewing permit and keys for 17 St. Leonard's Garden by claiming she wants to set up a women's boarding house. Upon inspecting the dismal, musty house, Frankie finds no personal belongings or traces of the Kemms, only an open "ABC Railway Guide" on a window seat. She concludes that the lack of evidence indicates the Kemms were being extremely careful regarding any illegal activities.
